lede: A look at November 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Prithvi Shaw and Adam Dunn, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: NYU Langone Health
events.desc: U.S. surgeons at NYU Langone Health announce the world's first whole eye transplant.

events.title: TAESA Flight 725
events.desc: TAESA Flight 725 crashes after takeoff from Uruapan International Airport in Uruapan, Michoacán, Mexico, killing all 18 people on board.

events.title: Garry Kasparov
events.desc: Garry Kasparov, 22, of the Soviet Union, becomes the youngest World Chess Champion by beating fellow Soviet Anatoly Karpov.

events.title: Power outage
events.desc: Several U.S. states and parts of Canada are hit by a series of blackouts lasting up to 13 hours in the Northeast blackout of 1965.

events.title: Kristallnacht
events.desc: Kristallnacht occurs, instigated by the Nazis using the killing of German diplomat Ernst vom Rath by Herschel Grynszpan as justification.

events.title: Second Sino-Japanese War
events.desc: Second Sino-Japanese War: The Chinese Army withdraws from the Battle of Shanghai.

events.title: Great Lakes Storm of 1913
events.desc: The Great Lakes Storm of 1913, the most destructive natural disaster ever to hit the lakes, reaches its greatest intensity after beginning two days earlier. The storm destroys 19 ships and kills more than 250 people.

events.title: Duke of Cornwall
events.desc: Prince George, Duke of Cornwall (later George V of the United Kingdom), becomes Prince of Wales and Earl of Chester.

events.title: Kentucky
events.desc: Kentucky marshals abduct abolitionist minister Calvin Fairbank from Jeffersonville, Indiana, and take him to Kentucky to stand trial for helping a slave escape.

events.title: Stockholm Bloodbath
events.desc: More than 50 people are sentenced and executed in the Stockholm Bloodbath.

births.name: Jim Riggleman
births.line: American baseball player, coach, and manager

deaths.text: Bobby Allison, American race car driver and businessman
Joe Walsh (Irish politician), Irish politician, Minister for Agriculture, Food and the Marine
John Campbell, 4th Duke of Argyll, Scottish general and politician

observances.text: Christian feast day: Dedication of the Archbasilica of St. John Lateran, Cathedral of the Pope (memorial feast day)
Christian feast day: Margery Kempe (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Theodore of Amasea (Roman Catholic Church)
